fault_hypothesis: The list comprehension on line 75 incorrectly includes points whose timestamp is exactly equal to the `end` boundary. The condition should use `< end` instead of `<= end` to implement the documented half-open interval.

constraints.intent: All points in the result must have timestamps strictly less than the end time.

spec.expr: len(__cbfl_result) == sum(1 for p in points if start <= p[0] < end)

constraints.intent: The number of returned points must equal the count satisfying the correct half-open interval.

spec.expr: not any(p[0] == end for p in points if start <= p[0])

constraints.intent: Of all points that pass the lower bound check, none should have a timestamp exactly at the end boundary.

spec.expr: all(isinstance(p, (tuple, list)) and len(p) == 2 for p in points)

constraints.intent: All elements in the input 'points' should be pairs (tuples or lists of length 2).
